UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores application-specific profile information for a user authenticated externally.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, matching the external authentication system's user ID.

email.description: The user's email address, obtained from the external authentication system.

name.description: The user's full name as provided during registration or from external authentication.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Property.title: Property
Property.description: Represents a rental property managed by the user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Property entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns or manages this property. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Property)

address.description: The full street address of the property.

propertyType.description: The type of the property (e.g., Flat, House, Bungalow, Maisonette, Studio, HMO).

status.description: The current occupancy status of the property (Vacant, Occupied, Under Maintenance).

numberOfBedrooms.description: The total number of bedrooms in the property.

numberOfBathrooms.description: The total number of bathrooms in the property.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the property record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the property record was last updated.

Tenancy.title: Tenancy
Tenancy.description: Details of a specific tenancy agreement for a property.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Tenancy entity.

propertyId.description: Reference to the Property this tenancy applies to. (Relationship: Property 1:N Tenancy)

tenantName.description: The full name of the tenant(s) for this tenancy.

tenantEmail.description: The primary email address of the tenant.

tenantTelephone.description: The primary telephone number of the tenant.

startDate.description: The start date of the tenancy agreement.

endDate.description: The scheduled end date of the tenancy agreement. Can be null if open-ended or not yet set.

notes.description: Any additional notes or specific terms related to this tenancy.

isActive.description: Indicates if this is the currently active tenancy for the property.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the tenancy record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the tenancy record was last updated.

PropertyImage.title: PropertyImage
PropertyImage.description: Stores information about an image uploaded for a property.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PropertyImage entity.

propertyId.description: Reference to the Property this image belongs to. (Relationship: Property 1:N PropertyImage)

imageUrl.description: The URL where the image is stored.

description.description: A brief description of what the image shows (e.g., 'Front view', 'Living room').

uploadedAt.description: Timestamp when the image was uploaded.

MaintenanceLog.title: MaintenanceLog
MaintenanceLog.description: Records a maintenance request or issue for a property.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MaintenanceLog entity.

propertyId.description: Reference to the Property the maintenance request is for. (Relationship: Property 1:N MaintenanceLog)

description.description: Detailed description of the maintenance issue.

urgencyLevel.description: The urgency level of the maintenance issue (e.g., Low, Medium, High, Critical).

status.description: The current status of the maintenance request (e.g., Open, In Progress, Resolved, Cancelled, Pending).

reportedAt.description: Timestamp when the maintenance issue was reported.

resolvedAt.description: Timestamp when the maintenance issue was resolved (optional).

notes.description: Internal notes about the maintenance process or contractor details.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the maintenance log record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the maintenance log record was last updated.

Inspection.title: Inspection
Inspection.description: Schedules and records details of a property inspection.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Inspection entity.

propertyId.description: Reference to the Property where the inspection is to take place. (Relationship: Property 1:N Inspection)

inspectionDate.description: The scheduled date and time for the inspection.

status.description: The current status of the inspection (e.g., Scheduled, Completed, Cancelled).

notes.description: Detailed findings and observations from the inspection.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the inspection record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the inspection record was last updated.

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Stores information and links to documents related to a property.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Document entity.

propertyId.description: Reference to the Property this document is associated with. (Relationship: Property 1:N Document)

documentType.description: The category or type of the document (e.g., Tenancy Agreement, Property Certificate, EPC, Gas Safety Certificate).

documentName.description: The display name of the document file.

documentUrl.description: The URL where the document file is stored.

uploadedAt.description: Timestamp when the document was uploaded.

expirationDate.description: The expiration date of the document, if applicable (e.g., for safety certificates).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the document record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the document record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores user profile information, directly identified by their authentication UID. The 'id' field within the document must match the '{userId}' in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the authenticated user, matching request.auth.uid.

definition.description: Represents a rental property managed by a specific user. The 'userId' field in the document must match the '{userId}' in the path for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the owner user.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the property.

definition.description: Details of a tenancy agreement for a specific property. Requires a denormalized 'userId' field in the document for authorization independence, matching the '{userId}' in the path. The 'propertyId' field in the document must match the '{propertyId}' in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the property owner.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent property.

params.name: tenancyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the tenancy record.

definition.description: Stores image information for a specific property. Requires a denormalized 'userId' field in the document for authorization independence, matching the '{userId}' in the path. The 'propertyId' field in the document must match the '{propertyId}' in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the property owner.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent property.

params.name: imageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the property image.

definition.description: Records maintenance requests for a specific property. Requires a denormalized 'userId' field in the document for authorization independence, matching the '{userId}' in the path. The 'propertyId' field in the document must match the '{propertyId}' in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the property owner.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent property.

params.name: logId
params.description: The unique identifier for the maintenance log entry.

definition.description: Schedules and records property inspections. Requires a denormalized 'userId' field in the document for authorization independence, matching the '{userId}' in the path. The 'propertyId' field in the document must match the '{propertyId}' in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the property owner.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent property.

params.name: inspectionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the inspection record.

definition.description: Stores document links and metadata for a specific property. Requires a denormalized 'userId' field in the document for authorization independence, matching the '{userId}' in the path. The 'propertyId' field in the document must match the '{propertyId}' in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the property owner.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent property.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the document record.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Path-Based Access Modeling to ensure robust security, scalability, and debuggability. The primary goal is to address the reported 'Missing or insufficient permissions' error by eliminating unauthorized top-level collection access and enforcing strict ownership-based data partitioning.

**Authorization Independence:** To prevent `get()` calls in security rules and enable atomic operations, authorization context is denormalized. Every entity, from `UserProfile` down to `Document`, is placed within a hierarchical path starting with `/users/{userId}`. This ensures that the owner's `userId` is always part of the document's path. Furthermore, for all subcollection documents (e.g., `Tenancy`, `PropertyImage`, `MaintenanceLog`, `Inspection`, `Document`), it is mandated that the document itself contains a denormalized `userId` field, matching the `{userId}` in its path. This redundant `userId` directly within the document, combined with its presence in the path, makes authorization rules simple (`request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` AND `request.auth.uid == userId` from the path wildcard) and atomic, as no cross-document `get()` operations are needed to determine ownership.

**Structural Segregation & QAPs:** All user-specific data is strictly segregated under `/users/{userId}`. This creates homogenous security postures for each collection. For example, a user's `properties` are entirely distinct from another user's `properties`. This structure inherently supports Queryable Authorization Policies (QAPs) because `list` operations are always performed within the scope of the authenticated user's ID. When a user requests `/users/{uid}/properties`, Firestore's default security behavior ensures that only documents within that specific subcollection are returned, thereby pre-filtering data by ownership. The original error, a `list` operation on a top-level `/tenants` collection, is resolved by *not creating* such a collection. Instead, `Tenancy` entities are stored as subcollections under specific properties: `/users/{userId}/properties/{propertyId}/tenancies/{tenancyId}`. This ensures that listing tenancies is always scoped to a specific property owned by the authenticated user, thus preventing broad, unauthorized data access and the need for complex, inefficient server-side filtering by rules.
